source_lists.title: Body Horror
source_lists.desc:
Body horror or biological horror might make up one of cinema’s most disgusting film subgenres in which the horror is principally derived from the graphic or psychologically disturbing violations of the human body. 

These violations may manifest through aberrant sex, mutations, mutilation, zombification, gratuitous violence, disease, decay, deformity, parasitism or unnatural movements of the body.

In this list I have rounded up some of the greatest body horror movies ever made. Some are full-blown horror movies, some are comedies, some are surrealist head trips, but all are prime examples of what cinema can be when gore goes wild.

source_lists.title: Body Horror
source_lists.desc:
In a nutshell: new configurations of the flesh.

Films that can be subsumed under the concept (not genre) of ‘body horror’. That is, films that deal with the horrific/disgusting representation of the human body, specifically focusing on its mutation, degeneration and/or destruction. 

The concept of body horror is fluctuating and variable, and it can take on different aesthetic forms: its most known and accepted one is that of the gruesome mutation (by means of diseases, supernatural forces, alien organisms etc.). To this respect, The Invasion of the Body Snatcher and acolytes are left out because of their specifically ‘vegetable’ nature (also because of the total absence of gore of the first one).

Sheer violence can also be part of the… more
